best.name: python-whois
best.maintained: active (port-43 WHOIS; output varies by registrar)
best.notes: Legacy port-43 WHOIS. Free-text, registrar-dependent, increasingly redacted (GDPR). Keep, but pair with RDAP for structured/normalized data.

best.name: RDAP (rdap.org bootstrap)
best.maintained: active (IETF standard, RFC 7480-7484; rdap.org redirects to the authoritative per-TLD RDAP server via IANA bootstrap)
best.notes: Successor to WHOIS. Returns standardized JSON: events (registration/expiry dates), entities (registrant/registrar w/ vCard), nameservers, status, secureDNS. rdap.org auto-routes to the correct registry RDAP endpoint. Parse JSON directly - no scraping. Best structured complement to the WHOIS we already have.

runner_up.name: lissy93/who-dat
runner_up.notes: Self-hostable service that merges RDAP+WHOIS into one normalized JSON shape, discovers RDAP server per-TLD via IANA bootstrap. Good if rdap.org normalization is insufficient. Public instance is best-effort; self-host for reliability.

capabilities.gaps: WHOIS history / historical registrant data is paid-only (SecurityTrails/WhoisXML). RDAP gives current state only.

best.name: dnspython resolver
best.notes: Covers core record types. Could add DoH fallback (Google/Cloudflare https://dns.google/resolve?name={selector}&type=A) for environments where UDP/53 is blocked, plus CAA/DNSKEY/DMARC TXT parsing. Adequate as-is.

runner_up.name: Google DNS-over-HTTPS
runner_up.notes: No-key JSON DoH endpoint; resilient when raw DNS is filtered. Cloudflare equivalent: https://cloudflare-dns.com/dns-query (Accept: application/dns-json).

capabilities.gaps: Passive DNS (historical A-record / IP-history, reverse-IP co-hosted domains) is not covered - that needs SecurityTrails/Censys/Netlas (keyed).

best.name: subfinder (ProjectDiscovery)
best.auth: none (works key-free; optional API keys in $HOME/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml unlock more sources)
best.notes: Best passive subdomain engine. Aggregates 30+ passive sources (crt.sh, Anubis, HackerTarget, Wayback, etc.). Runs fully passive with no keys; -json emits one object/line {host,source}. Go binary - add an availability check in the runner.

best.name: crt.sh CT search
best.notes: Already wired. Doubles as a passive subdomain source via cert SANs - overlaps with one of subfinder's sources but is a reliable key-free standalone.

runner_up.name: theHarvester
runner_up.install: pipx install theHarvester  (or git clone + pipx install --python python3.12 .)
runner_up.notes: Org-recon multitool: subdomains + emails + hosts + virtual hosts in one pass. Most actively maintained of this class (v4.x, 2026; flit-core packaging). JSON output. Prefer subfinder for pure subdomain breadth; use theHarvester when you also want emails/hosts for a company investigation. Pin sources that need no key.

capabilities.gaps: Active DNS brute-force (amass active / puredns) and resolver-verified live subdomains not covered by the passive picks; amass active mode is heavier and noisier. Censys/Netlas give more SANs but are keyed/credit-limited.

best.name: crt.sh
best.notes: Already wired. The canonical free CT log search. Yields SAN domains, issuer, validity windows. Can be slow/flaky under load; add retry + timeout (already 30s).

runner_up.name: SSLMate Cert Spotter API
runner_up.notes: Clean JSON CT API, good crt.sh fallback. Free unauthenticated tier is rate-limited (a handful of queries/hr); add API key for more. Use as failover when crt.sh times out.

capabilities.gaps: None significant for free CT search - crt.sh + Cert Spotter cover it. Pre-cert/expired-only filtering is manual.

best.name: Wayback Machine CDX API
best.notes: Already wired. CDX index of historical snapshots. For URL-discovery add collapse=urlkey and matchType=prefix to dedup; raise limit for full crawl history.

runner_up.name: gau (getallurls, lc/gau)
runner_up.notes: Aggregates known historical URLs from Wayback + Common Crawl + AlienVault OTX + urlscan in one pass - broader than Wayback alone. Key-free. Great for exposed-path / endpoint discovery. JSON output. Add an availability check (Go binary).

capabilities.gaps: archive.today (archive.ph) has no stable public API and is Cloudflare-gated - manual/excluded. Common Crawl direct index queries are heavy; gau abstracts them.

best.name: urlscan.io Search API
best.auth: none (search is key-free; submitting new scans needs a free API key)
best.notes: Already wired. Searches existing public scans -> resolved IPs, servers, page domains, screenshots. To trigger a fresh scan use POST https://urlscan.io/api/v1/scan/ with API-Key header (free tier ~100 private/1000 public scans/day).

runner_up.name: ThreatFox (abuse.ch) IOC search
runner_up.notes: Already wired. Checks domain/url/IP/hash against malware IOC DB. Note: abuse.ch now issues free Auth-Keys (Auth-Key header) for some endpoints - verify ThreatFox still accepts anonymous POST, register a free key if it starts 401ing.

capabilities.gaps: Live rendered-DOM/redirect-chain capture beyond urlscan (e.g. a headless browser scan) is out of scope for the structured line. VirusTotal URL report (keyed, 4 req/min free) could add multi-engine verdicts.

best.name: HTTP header probe (requests)
best.notes: Already wired. Captures Server header + resolves IP. Minimal fingerprinting (header-only). Good cheap baseline; upgrade path below for real tech-stack detection.

best.name: httpx (ProjectDiscovery)
best.notes: Best structured fingerprinter. -tech-detect uses Wappalyzer signatures -> CMS/framework/server/CDN; -json emits one record with status, title, tech[], webserver, cname, host(IP), tls. Single-host probe is fast. Go binary - add availability check. Big upgrade over header-only http_headers.

runner_up.name: WhatWeb
runner_up.install: gem install whatweb  (or apt install whatweb)
runner_up.notes: Mature Ruby fingerprinter, 1800+ plugins, aggression-tunable, JSON log. Heavier dependency (Ruby) than httpx. Use if httpx tech coverage misses something; otherwise httpx is the lighter single-binary choice.

capabilities.gaps: Passive-only tech detection (no live request) requires BuiltWith/Wappalyzer API (keyed). httpx/whatweb both touch the target.

best.name: Google Dork Generator
best.notes: Already wired. Emits dork strings (index-of, filetype:pdf/xlsx, inurl:admin/login, 'api key'/'password'/'secret', pastebin/github mentions). NOTE: it only GENERATES queries - it does not execute them. Pair with the web-search line or a Programmable Search API to actually run them.

best.name: gau (getallurls)
best.notes: Best STRUCTURED exposed-path discovery: surfaces historically-known URLs (from Wayback/CommonCrawl/OTX/urlscan) including stale .env/.git/backup/admin endpoints that current crawls miss. Post-filter the URL list for sensitive extensions/paths. Doubles in the web_archive capability.

runner_up.name: TruffleHog
runner_up.install: pip install trufflehog  (or brew install trufflehog)
runner_up.notes: Verified-secret scanner (700+ detectors, live credential validation). Repo/filesystem/GitHub-org scoped, NOT domain-scoped - only relevant once a domain pivots to a GitHub org/repo. JSON output, very active. Out of the direct domain->web flow but the best leaked-credential verifier when a code surface is found.

capabilities.gaps: Actually EXECUTING dorks needs a search API (Brave/Bing/Google Programmable Search, all keyed) - the generator only produces query strings. GitHub code-search for the domain string (api.github.com/search/code, keyed) would find leaked configs referencing the domain; worth wiring separately.

excluded.reason: Passive-DNS/subdomain/WHOIS-history would be ideal but free tier is only 50 queries/MONTH and ownership moved to Recorded Future (pricing/API instability flagged in audit). Not viable for automated use; revisit only if a key is provisioned.

Legacy free Search disabled in 2026; new Platform requires Starter+ tier for subdomain/host search (credit-metered, ~5 credits/100 results). No usable free automation tier. Keep on backlog as keyed HTTP collector.

Excellent (response-body regex search Shodan can't do) but Community free tier is only 50 requests/day and requires an API key. Mark to_wire later as keyed HTTP collector; not free/no-key enough to be a default pick now.
Domain/DNS endpoints need a paid membership; only InternetDB (IP-only) is free, which lives in the ip bucket, not domain_web.
archive.today/archive.ph has no stable public API and is Cloudflare/anti-bot gated - manual-only, not automatable in the structured line.

Web UI only (CSRF-token + HTML scraping, no documented stable JSON API); hashemilab acquisition added a keyed API but free programmatic use is unreliable. subfinder/crt.sh cover the same passive subdomain ground key-free.
OSS abandoned after Intel 471 acquisition (2+ yrs stale per audit). We build our own orchestration; do not depend on it.

Official Wappalyzer CLI/npm package was deprecated/went commercial; signatures live on but the standalone OSS CLI is unmaintained. httpx -tech-detect uses Wappalyzer fingerprints and is the maintained path.
